---
title: "9. Conclusion (open)"
---

> **Deliberately open.** Under CIS, conclusions are earned through evidence. At v0.1 the evidence is
> synthetic and the confidence grades are C/D, so this chapter records *state and next steps*, not
> verdicts.

## What this paper has (and has not) established

- **Has:** a governed method (CIS), a structured set of falsifiable claims, illustrative frameworks
  that make those claims measurable, and a test suite guarding the machinery.
- **Has not:** validated *any* of the three decision questions. No segment, positioning, or GTM
  strategy is recommended yet.

## Current state of the three decision questions

| Question | Current state | Gate to advance |
|---|---|---|
| Q1 — First segment | Illustrative ranking only (grade C) | Discovery/pilot evidence for top segments (Ch. 5) |
| Q2 — Positioning | Framing scored, untested on buyers (grade C) | Message-testing results (Ch. 4) |
| Q3 — Evidence threshold | Threshold defined (grade B gate) | Instrument the register; meet the gate (Ch. 7) |

## The central thesis

The proposal's claim that the CRM is the foundation for AI readiness (**H-CRM-AI**) remains a
hypothesis (grade D→C). The planned pilot (`EV-010`) is the pivotal next experiment.

## What "done" looks like

A conclusion is warranted when the relevant claims reach the confidence grade their decisions require
(≥ B for a GTM commitment), with reproducible evidence in the register and a green test suite. Until
then, this chapter stays open — by design.

## Immediate next steps

1. Run the AI-readiness pilot (`EV-010`) and record outcomes (Ch. 6).
2. Run discovery calls for the top two segments (Ch. 5) and a message test for positioning (Ch. 4).
3. Re-run the dashboards and the evidence summary; let the numbers move.
4. Revisit this chapter only when evidence — not assertion — supports a verdict.
